skill.name: team-composition-analysis
skill.description: Helps founders plan startup team structure, hiring priorities, compensation ranges, equity allocation, and org chart evolution from pre-seed through Series A.
skill.summary: Plan startup headcount, compensation, equity, and reporting structures by stage.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown formatting and startup planning prose. No executable code, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt was found. Low risk remains because the skill references companion resources that are not present in the submitted file set.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Content
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences, inline file references, or plain organizational examples. The reported weak cryptography and network reconnaissance patterns are ordinary startup planning terms, salary ranges, role names, or org chart text. No command execution, cryptographic operation, network scan, or malicious intent was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the flagged locations are documentation text and Markdown examples, not executable code. The skill file contains no scripts, shell invocations, network instructions, or cryptographic API usage.

low_findings.title: Missing Referenced Companion Materials
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to open detailed examples and reference materials, but the submitted file set only contains SKILL.md. This is a quality and reliability issue rather than a security threat.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file structure lists only SKILL.md, while these lines refer to companion materials. The impact is limited to incomplete guidance because no unsafe behavior is introduced.

content.value_statement: Early startup hiring plans often miss stage, budget, equity, and reporting constraints. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create practical headcount plans from pre-seed through Series A.
actual_capabilities: Maps team size and department mix across pre-seed, seed, and Series A stages.
Suggests role-by-role hiring priorities for engineering, sales, product, customer success, and operations.
Provides US salary ranges, fully loaded compensation guidance, and geographic adjustment factors.
Explains equity allocation ranges for founders, early employees, and later-stage hires.
Outlines reporting structures, span of control, and hiring velocity assumptions.
Creates budget planning guidance using headcount, compensation, and start date assumptions.
limitations: Salary benchmarks are presented as 2024 US ranges and may need current market validation.
It does not replace legal, tax, compensation, or HR advice.
It focuses on early-stage startups from pre-seed through Series A.
Referenced companion files are not included in the submitted package.

use_cases.title: Build a Seed Hiring Plan
use_cases.description: Create a prioritized hiring roadmap with roles, timing, cost, and equity ranges for a seed-stage startup.

use_cases.title: Validate Headcount Budget
use_cases.description: Estimate fully loaded payroll cost and compare team plans against revenue stage and burn constraints.

use_cases.title: Design an Org Chart
use_cases.description: Plan reporting lines, department growth, and manager ratios as the team scales from founders to functional leaders.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Team Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan the first startup team for a pre-seed company with this product, budget, and founder skill set: [details].

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Seed Hires
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a seed-stage hiring plan for a B2B SaaS startup with [ARR], [runway], [team size], and [growth goals].

prompt_templates.title: Model Compensation and Equity
prompt_templates.prompt: Estimate salary, fully loaded cost, and equity ranges for these planned roles: [roles]. Include assumptions and tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Org Design Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare two org chart options for scaling from [current headcount] to [target headcount]. Evaluate cost, speed, risk, and management load.

output_examples.input: Plan hires for a seed-stage SaaS company with ten employees and twelve months of runway.
output: A prioritized hiring sequence by quarter.
Role recommendations for engineering, sales, product, and operations.
Estimated salary, loaded cost, and equity ranges for each role.
Risks related to runway, hiring velocity, and management capacity.

output_examples.input: Design an org chart for a Series A company growing from 18 to 45 people.
output: A department structure with reporting lines.
Recommended manager ratios and leadership roles.
Timing guidance for adding VP, director, and recruiting capacity.
Notes on coordination risks as functions expand.

best_practices: Provide stage, revenue, runway, current headcount, and founder strengths before asking for a plan.
Ask for assumptions and tradeoffs when using compensation, equity, or hiring velocity estimates.
Validate salary, equity, legal, and tax details with current market data and professional advisers.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the benchmark ranges as guaranteed current compensation data.
Do not use the output as a final legal, tax, or employment policy document.
Do not scale every department evenly when one function is the main business constraint.

faq.question: What startup stages does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers pre-seed, seed, and Series A team planning.

faq.question: Can it calculate a full hiring budget?
faq.answer: It can estimate fully loaded costs from role counts, salaries, timing, benefits, and overhead assumptions.

faq.question: Does it include equity allocation guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides stage-based equity ranges for founders, early employees, senior hires, and later-stage roles.

faq.question: Is the compensation data current?
faq.answer: The skill labels its salary benchmarks as 2024 US ranges, so users should verify current market data.

faq.question: Can it create an org chart?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline reporting structures, span of control, and department buildout by stage.

faq.question: Does it execute code or access external services?
faq.answer: No. The submitted skill is a Markdown guidance file and contains no executable code or network calls.
